content: I will modify the client `all_pujas.tsx` in Expo to dynamically load the View All banner and header configuration from the `website_settings` table. I'll also add a real-time listener so changes made in the admin panel are immediately reflected on the mobile client.
thinking:
**Examining Supabase Integration**

I'm now diving into the `all_pujas.tsx` file, specifically looking at how the Supabase client is integrated on line 17. My focus is now on implementing dynamic settings retrieval within that component. I will be looking closely at the existing structure to identify the best places to incorporate this new functionality, prioritizing how to handle fetching and any potential error conditions.


**Implementing Dynamic Settings**

I've been analyzing the `all_pujas.tsx` component. I've realized that the `viewAllSettings.banner_image` can be both a remote URL or a local asset. I've confirmed that `expo-image` supports both types of sources, as it can accept either a URL string or an object with a URI. I'm preparing to craft a loading method in the existing `useEffect` to handle these different source types.


**Designing Settings Loading**

Now, I'm thinking about where to load the settings, deciding between `loadGeneralPoojas` or a new function/hook. I've realized that the ideal place is now with real-time updates through subscribing to changes in `website_settings`. My plan is to load `view_all_settings` from the `website_settings` table and subscribe to `postgres_changes` on the same table. This will allow for dynamic real-time client updates when the admin makes changes to the banner, title, or subheading. I am preparing to implement this approach.


**Refining Realtime Subscription**

I've decided on the subscription implementation. Listening to all events on the `website_settings` table and filtering within the callback is the safest and most reliable method across Supabase versions, so I've implemented that. I'm now examining the logic within the `postgres_changes` callback to make sure that the `viewAllSettings` object is correctly updated. I've successfully implemented the subscription and am ready to address the image source.

\n\n
I'm now implementing the banner image loading logic. I'm dynamically choosing the image source. I'm constructing a source based on `viewAllSettings.banner_image`. If the value starts with 'http', I treat it as a remote URL; otherwise, I use a local fallback. I've ensured a robust solution to fetch it locally or remotely!


**Addressing Text Styles**

I'm now focusing on the text elements within the section header. I need to make some formatting changes to the heading, subheading, and icon. I've realized that the `#checkmark-circle` hex color value is a typo, and am now changing it to a correct color. I'm also preparing to make the title dynamic by connecting it to the settings.


**Combining Settings Fetching**

I'm now integrating settings fetching with the existing `useEffect`. I will consolidate settings loading and the realtime channel subscription into this existing hook for better code organization. I am implementing the changes so the settings and the subscriptions work in sync. Now, the goal is to keep the code organized and avoid redundancy!


**Refactoring Product Fetching**

I'm now integrating the pooja product loading logic and the Supabase realtime channel into the `useEffect` hook. I will be consolidating these separate functionalities to optimize for code organization. The goal is to simplify this component, while simultaneously handling data fetching and real-time updates seamlessly in one block. I'll test the new streamlined process.


**Consolidating Data Fetching**

I'm working on the `all_pujas.tsx` component, now with the goal of integrating multiple edits using `multi_replace_file_content`. I will first modify the state and the `useEffect` hook, replacing lines 133-178. My next plan is to update the component to load the pooja data and include the necessary settings. I am implementing this data structure by updating the file in multiple blocks!

args.Description: "Modify all_pujas.tsx to dynamically load the store's view-all page banner, badge text, title, and subheadings from Supabase, with real-time settings update subscriptions."
args.Instruction: "Implement dynamic fetching of view_all_settings inside useEffect and update rendering sections."
